author:	sourcery-ai
association:	none
edited:	true
status:	none
--
<!-- Generated by sourcery-ai[bot]: start review_guide -->

## Reviewer's Guide

Updates GitHub workflows so releases are manually triggered, only run when there are meaningful code changes, use a new v2 date-based tagging scheme, refreshes GoReleaser and golangci-lint actions, and introduces a dedicated test workflow for main and WorkingCLI branches.

#### Sequence diagram for manual release workflow with change detection

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  actor Developer
  participant GitHubActions
  participant TestJob
  participant ReleaseJob
  participant CheckChanges
  participant TagStep
  participant GoReleaser

  Developer->>GitHubActions: Dispatch release.yml (workflow_dispatch, force_release)
  GitHubActions->>TestJob: Run tests
  TestJob-->>GitHubActions: Report result (success/failure)

  GitHubActions->>ReleaseJob: Start release job if always() and (tests success or force_release)

  ReleaseJob->>CheckChanges: Run Check for Changes step
  alt force_release is true
    CheckChanges-->>ReleaseJob: skip=false
  else force_release is false
    CheckChanges->>CheckChanges: Fetch tags and find LAST_TAG
    CheckChanges->>CheckChanges: git diff LAST_TAG..HEAD
    alt Meaningful changes detected
      CheckChanges-->>ReleaseJob: skip=false
    else No meaningful changes detected
      CheckChanges-->>ReleaseJob: skip=true
    end
  end

  opt skip is false
    ReleaseJob->>TagStep: Generate Version Tag v2.YYYYMMDD.N[-branch]
    TagStep-->>ReleaseJob: version output

    ReleaseJob->>TagStep: Tag commit and push tag to origin

    ReleaseJob->>GoReleaser: Run goreleaser release --clean with GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG
    GoReleaser-->>ReleaseJob: Build and publish release artifacts
  end
```

#### Flow diagram for v2 date-based version tag generation

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Start Generate Version Tag step] --> B[Set TODAY_INT and PREFIX v2.YYYYMMDD]
  B --> C{Is branch main?}
  C -->|Yes| D[Set SUFFIX empty and REGEX ^PREFIX\.N$]
  C -->|No| E[Sanitize branch to SAFE_BRANCH, set SUFFIX -SAFE_BRANCH and REGEX ^PREFIX\.N-SUFFIX$]
  D --> F[Fetch tags matching PREFIX*]
  E --> F
  F --> G[Iterate matching tags, filter by REGEX, track MAX_N]
  G --> H{Is MAX_N equal to -1?}
  H -->|Yes| I[Set NEXT_N = 1]
  H -->|No| J[Set NEXT_N = MAX_N + 1]
  I --> K[Compose FINAL_VER = PREFIX.NEXT_N + SUFFIX]
  J --> K
  K --> L[Write version=FINAL_VER to GitHub step output]
  L --> M[End Generate Version Tag step]
```

### File-Level Changes

| Change | Details | Files |
| ------ | ------- | ----- |
| Change release workflow trigger and force-release semantics to be manual and based on meaningful changes rather than test failures. | <ul><li>Remove push-based triggers so the release workflow is only started via workflow_dispatch.</li><li>Rename the force_release input description to indicate it overrides the no-meaningful-changes guard instead of test failures.</li><li>Make the release job condition multiline but keep the logic that allows forcing a release even when tests fail.</li></ul> | `.github/workflows/release.yml` |
| Add a pre-release check that skips tagging and GoReleaser when there are no meaningful changes since the last tag. | <ul><li>Introduce a Check for Changes step that optionally short-circuits the workflow via a skip output.</li><li>Detect the last tag, accounting for manually tagged HEAD commits, and diff changed files against it.</li><li>Treat only non-doc, non-meta paths as meaningful; skip the release steps when only ignored paths have changed.</li></ul> | `.github/workflows/release.yml` |
| Replace the date-based tagging scheme with a v2.YYYYMMDD.N[-branch] versioning model and wire it into GoReleaser. | <ul><li>Generate tags using a v2.YYYYMMDD.N pattern with an optional sanitized branch suffix for non-main branches.</li><li>Scan existing tags matching the current date and scope to compute the next daily build counter.</li><li>Gate tag creation and GoReleaser execution on the skip flag and pass the computed tag explicitly via GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG, updating the GoReleaser action to the v2 series.</li></ul> | `.github/workflows/release.yml` |
| Update lint workflow formatting and use a newer golangci-lint GitHub Action. | <ul><li>Normalize the push branches array formatting.</li><li>Bump golangci-lint GitHub Action from v3 to v66 while still using the latest linter binary.</li></ul> | `.github/workflows/lint.yml` |
| Introduce a dedicated test workflow for main and WorkingCLI branches. | <ul><li>Add a new test.yml workflow that runs on push and pull_request to main and WorkingCLI.</li><li>Configure the job to check out the repo, set up Go from go.mod, and execute go test -v ./....</li></ul> | `.github/workflows/test.yml` |

---

<details>
<summary>Tips and commands</summary>

#### Interacting with Sourcery

- **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request.
- **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments.
- **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an
  issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a
  review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it.
- **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull
  request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment
  `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time.
- **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in
  the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you
  want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to
  (re-)generate the summary at any time.
- **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull
  request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time.
- **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the
  pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already
  addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore.
- **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull
  request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you
  want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment
  `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review!

#### Customizing Your Experience

Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to:
- Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request
  summary, the reviewer's guide, and others.
- Change the review language.
- Add, remove or edit custom review instructions.
- Adjust other review settings.

#### Getting Help

- [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback.
- Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information.
- Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

</details>

<!-- Generated by sourcery-ai[bot]: end review_guide -->
--
author:	sourcery-ai
association:	none
edited:	false
status:	commented
--
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- In the `lint.yml` workflow, `golangci/golangci-lint-action@v66` looks like a typo and will not resolve to a valid tag; consider pinning to the current major (e.g. `@v6`) or a specific released version.
- For the longer bash snippets in `release.yml` (e.g. the `Check for Changes` and `Generate Version Tag` steps), adding `set -euo pipefail` at the top of each script block would make failures more obvious and reduce the risk of silently ignored errors.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In the `lint.yml` workflow, `golangci/golangci-lint-action@v66` looks like a typo and will not resolve to a valid tag; consider pinning to the current major (e.g. `@v6`) or a specific released version.
- For the longer bash snippets in `release.yml` (e.g. the `Check for Changes` and `Generate Version Tag` steps), adding `set -euo pipefail` at the top of each script block would make failures more obvious and reduce the risk of silently ignored errors.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path=".github/workflows/lint.yml" line_range="20" />
<code_context>
           go-version-file: 'go.mod'
       - name: golangci-lint
-        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v3
+        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v66
         with:
           version: latest
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue (bug_risk):** The golangci-lint GitHub Action version tag `v66` is likely incorrect and will fail to resolve.

This action only publishes low major version tags (e.g. `v3`, `v4`, `v5`, `v6`); `v66` does not exist and will cause the workflow to fail. Please update to a valid major tag (such as `@v6`) or pin to a specific commit SHA for reproducibility.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

***
